design_philosophy.core_loop: Introduce and adjust policies across seven governance areas (tax, economy, welfare, foreign policy, transport, law/order, public services), manage political capital, navigate voter factions, win elections through promise-keeping and faction satisfaction
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing competing voter factions with irreconcilable interests; maintaining budget while satisfying diverse groups; keeping election promises versus pragmatic governance
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a democratic leader, shape national policy, understand complex socio-political systems, experience consequences of governance decisions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces that perfect voter satisfaction is impossible—players belong to multiple overlapping factions with conflicting priorities. Election promises create accountability. Budget constraints and political capital spending create resource tension. Cynicism mechanic punishes broken promises, treating voter trust as measurable consequence

difficulty.continues_system: Elections serve as natural checkpoints; loss of election is primary failure state
difficulty.checkpointing: Turn-based progression with elections as major milestones
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can adjust policy complexity and select different nations with varying initial conditions; sandbox nature allows self-imposed challenge levels

progression.upgrade_system: Policy adjustments via sliders controlling government funding, tax rates, and regulation levels; political capital earned from loyal ministers gates major policy shifts
progression.unlock_mechanism: Nations selectable at start; policies available from game start but gated by political capital and budget constraints
replayability_hooks: Multiple selectable nations with different starting conditions
Randomized events, dilemmas, and situations each playthrough
Voter cynicism persists across turns, creating long-term consequences
Different faction compositions reward varied political strategies
Open modding support enables custom countries and mechanics

world_building_techniques: Faction-based voter archetypes (religious, patriotic, parents, capitalists, socialists, liberals, conservatives) embedded in simulation
Events and dilemmas presented as contextual challenges (disease outbreaks, judge appointments, pollution crises)
Economic and social statistics reflect policy consequences
Nations represent distinct political contexts (Democracy 2 uses fictional nations; later games add real countries with unique dynamics)
Voter overlapping memberships (Poor Conservative Smoker Patriot) create emergent narrative complexity

danger_signals: Broken election promises increase voter cynicism
Unaddressed situations (pollution, homelessness, petrol protests) destabilize voter support
Cabinet minister loyalty affects political capital availability
Budget debt accumulation constrains policy flexibility
environmental_storytelling: Crime rates reflect law/order policy effectiveness
Air quality responds to transport and economy policies
Unemployment correlates with economy and welfare settings
Public services quality tied to budget allocation
Tax evasion levels indicate voter dissatisfaction with taxation
